channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-1680] LBUG cl_lock.c:1949:discard_cb()) (ORI-726)
item.description:
Client LBUGS - This appears to be the same issue as ORI-726, but reporting separately just in case, as ORI is PPC issue.
------------

Jul 25 17:27:40 ehyperion790 kernel: Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == recovery-mds-scale test failover_ost: failover OST == 17:27:36 (1343262456)
Jul 25 17:27:43 ehyperion790 kernel: LustreError: 13860:0:(cl_lock.c:1949:discard_cb()) ASSERTION( (!(page->cp_type == CPT_CACHEABLE) || (!PageDirty(cl_page_vmpage(env, page)))) ) failed:
Jul 25 17:27:43 ehyperion790 kernel: LustreError: 13860:0:(cl_lock.c:1949:discard_cb()) ASSERTION( (!(page->cp_type == CPT_CACHEABLE) || (!PageDirty(cl_page_vmpage(env, page)))) ) failed:
Jul 25 17:27:48 ehyperion790 kernel: LustreError: 13860:0:(cl_lock.c:1949:discard_cb()) LBUG
Jul 25 17:27:48 ehyperion790 kernel: LustreError: 13860:0:(cl_lock.c:1949:discard_cb()) LBUG

-----------

25 17:28:36 ehyperion790 kernel: Kernel panic - not syncing: LBUG

item.environment: LLNL Hyperion, running the OST failover portion of recovery-scale - failure occurs close to the start of the test.

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Sorry, my reading of the summary was too quick. I thought this was just a renamed ORI-726 so the patch could land to master, and that it was definitely related to PPC. I've removed the ppc label.
Duplicate of LU-1442.
Oh, I see, this should be resolved as duplicate of LU-1442. I'll do that.
Reopening. Somehow this ticket and ORI-726 were both marked "resolved" as duplicates of each other. One needs to stay open until the issue is resolved.

LU-1442 can definitely cause this issue, let me explain here:
1. from the description of lu-1442, one page can be wrongly marked as Dirty but not be put into OSC's write cache;
2. when the lock is canceled, we only cleanup the region of osc write cache covered by the lock, however, the page is not in osc write cache as I stated in 1;
3. when the page is discarded, we verify that there is no dirty page in the lock region by checking vmpage dirty flag. This will hit assertion.

As a conclusion, I think these are different symptoms of the same issue.
Correct me if I'm wrong, but I think LU-1442 is a separate issue. I thought Jinshan discovered the root cause of this issue when investigating LU-1442, not that they are the same bug?
ok so let's mark this as a duplicate of LU-1442 then and reopen if that proves incorrect
Hi Jinshan, What's the status of this? Is http://review.whamcloud.com/3447 all that is needed to fix the assertion?
This patch is landed to master and so should be in the next tag

Recently I found a problem at LU-1442 where a page was wrongly marked as dirty but not in osc writing cache. That will definitely cause this kind of issue because osc_cache_writeback_range() won't clean up the whole lock region.

Based on the situation that there is no enough log I'd like to try that patch first. The patch is located at: http://review.whamcloud.com/3447
